title: Podman cp dereferences symlinks in host context
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When copying into a path in a container that contains a symlink, the symlink is resolved on the host, not within the container, 

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman run -t -i --name testctr --rm fedora bash`

2. Inside the container: `ln -s /tmp /test`

3. Outside the container: `touch testfile; podman cp testfile testctr:/test`

4. In the container, `ls /tmp`

5. Outside the container, `ls /tmp` 

**Describe the results you received:**

Testfile is created outside the container

**Describe the results you expected:**

Testfile is created inside the container

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

Latest master

comments.body: We also need to pause containers while copying into them to avoid CVE-2018-15664

CVE-2019-10152 has been assigned for this issue:

https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1715667
Hey there, do you think we can create a new release which contains the CVE fix? :innocent:

Waiting for the additional upstream changes to chrootarchive, then we can
cut a release once those land in c/storage and get vendored here
